headline: Malaysian Peacekeepers Report 28 Safety Incidents Abroad
context: Malaysian armed forces personnel on overseas peacekeeping missions reported 28 safety incidents in 2025 the highest in a decade.

cards.big: 28 safety incidents for Malaysian peacekeepers abroad highest in decade
cards.sub: Incidents in Lebanon Somalia and Western Sahara involved hostile fire and IEDs

cards.big: 8 personnel wounded requiring medical evacuation to Malaysian military hospital
cards.sub: Lebanon deployment accounted for 18 of 28 incidents due to regional escalation

cards.big: Defence Ministry enhanced personal protective equipment after Q2 review
cards.sub: RM45 million allocated for upgraded armoured vehicles for peacekeeping units

cards.big: Parliamentary oversight committee reviewed all 28 incidents in closed session
cards.sub: Committee recommended additional hazard pay and family support for deployed personnel

cards.big: Peacekeeping risks test whether Malaysia adequately protects and compensates deployed personnel
28 peacekeeping safety incidents abroad. Troop-contributing nations must negotiate better force protection terms.
